rows.definition: ctxplan.Materialize re-derives a budgeted View of the lossless context store each turn: PlanCells picks which spans fit the budget, the selected ones are paged in through the trust gate, and a faithfulness Witness reconciles rendered-vs-refused so every elided span stays recoverable.
rows.distinction: Not the per-id Store.Materialize page-in of a single span's bytes, and not the model.materialize*Tensors manifest rewrite; it is the whole-history context-View builder.

rows.definition: ctxplan.MaterializeLayout is the layout-aware peer of Materialize: it uses a four-area Layout to choose the bounded candidate set and per-area precision, then renders the selected exact spans through the same trust gate and witness reconciliation as the default path.
rows.distinction: Not the default budget-only Materialize (which takes no Layout) and not the model tensor materializers; it is the Layout-parameterized context-View builder.

rows.definition: cachemeta.MaterializeVerdict is the central cache gate: given the runtime materialization view, the stored Entry, the requested key, and quality evidence, it returns a LookupVerdict deciding whether a cached prefix/KV span may be served (Hit) or must be refused (Miss).
rows.distinction: Not the ctxplan Materialize that builds a context View — MaterializeVerdict renders no spans; it returns a serve/refuse decision on a cached entry.

rows.definition: memview.Materializer is the producer type whose MaterializeSnippet turns a (source page, offset, length) selector into a provenance-bound MemoryViewRecord plus an admission Verdict, filling the record's Body only when the source page is trusted (Allow) and leaving it nil on a tainted source (Quarantine).
rows.distinction: Not the Materialize verb or the model tensor materializers; it is a stateful view-record producer that gates snippet admission on source taint.

rows.definition: materializeFalconTensors and its per-architecture siblings rewrite a loaded source/GGUF tensor manifest into fak's canonical tensor names (aliasing and splitting source-format tensors) for one model family before inference.
rows.distinction: Not the ctxplan/cachemeta Materialize (which build or serve a context view) — these mutate the model's load-time tensor manifest, one function per architecture (falcon/gpt-neox/gpt-oss/mpt/mixtral/qwen3.5/minimax shared-experts).

rows.definition: materializeTensorAliases applies the config's explicit canonical->source tensor-name aliases, creating zero-copy canonical manifest rows before any fused-tensor split, so a loader can point a canonical fused tensor at a source-format fused name.
rows.distinction: Not the per-architecture materialize*Tensors functions (each hard-codes one family's name map); this is the generic, config-driven aliasing step shared across all architectures.

rows.definition: RenderTurn (on the agent CtxViewPlanner / SessionPlanner) re-renders a turn's message history through the ctxplan seam — materializing a budgeted View and paging resident spans back as the agent message list — and returns its input unchanged when the seam is disabled.
rows.distinction: Not renderTurnDebugStats (a debug telemetry line about a served turn); RenderTurn produces the actual turn message list handed to the model.

rows.definition: ctxplan View.RenderedTokens sums the token cost of every span actually paged into the fresh history — the realized resident size, which is <= budget by construction.
rows.distinction: Not RenderedBytes (a raw byte count of paged-in cells); RenderedTokens is the 4-bytes/token estimate total of a ctxplan View's rendered spans.

rows.definition: RenderedBytes (json rendered_bytes, in memq Result.Stats and contextq) is the stat counting the total bytes an OpRender / context query paged into context, accumulated across rendered cells.
rows.distinction: Not RenderedTokens (a ctxplan View's token estimate); RenderedBytes is the raw byte tally materialized by a memq/contextq render op.

rows.definition: memq.OpRender is the query op that materializes the selected cell set into context as a read-only page-in through the trust gate, tallying RenderedBytes.
rows.distinction: Not OpScan (which (re)loads/enumerates cells) — OpRender is the terminal page-in step that produces RenderItem cells and never mutates the store.

rows.definition: agent.RenderTrace renders the per-call agent trace log — each tool call's arm, turn, args, verdict, adjudicator, reason and disposition — as a human text report.
rows.distinction: Not the RenderMarkdown bench-report renderers; RenderTrace emits the per-call adjudication trace of a single agent run, not a benchmark comparison.

rows.definition: gateway.renderVerdict projects a folded abi.Verdict (plus the admitted result's Meta) onto the wire WireVerdict shape — naming the kind, refusal reason, adjudicator, and disposition for the HTTP client.
rows.distinction: Not cachemeta.MaterializeVerdict (a cache serve/refuse decision); renderVerdict serializes an already-decided kernel verdict to the wire.

rows.definition: gateway Server.renderTurnDebugStats emits one per-turn debug key=val line (trace, wire, token counts, cache read/create, reset-health) when the --debug-stats sink is wired, and is a byte-identical no-op when it is off.
rows.distinction: Not RenderTurn (which builds the model's turn message list) — this only formats a debug telemetry line about an already-served turn.

rows.definition: gateway Server.renderMetrics renders the gateway's full Prometheus exposition text — the metric families scraped at /metrics.
rows.distinction: Not renderTurnDebugStats (a single per-turn debug line); renderMetrics emits the whole Prometheus snapshot for the scrape endpoint.

rows.definition: accounts.Registry.RenderView is a pure function projecting the account registry into the YAML text of a named view (dos/job), so `check` can diff on-disk bytes against a freshly rendered view and `sync` can write them.
rows.distinction: Not the ctxplan View / RenderTurn rendering of model context; RenderView renders an accounts-registry projection to YAML.

rows.definition: The RenderMarkdown family (benchscore, swebench, terminalbench, webbench, agenticbench, the browseraction action-mediation report, ...) renders a completed Report/Comparison struct of measured results into a Markdown document.
rows.distinction: Not the *ContractMarkdown renderers (which emit a pre-run promise before any number exists); RenderMarkdown emits measured results after a run.

rows.definition: RenderOfficialRunContractMarkdown renders a benchmark's official-run CONTRACT to Markdown — the pre-run promise (target choice, evidence class, claim boundary, whether a result claim is allowed); siblings render the DeepSWE and opus-smoke SWE-bench contracts and the terminalbench rehearsal preflight.
rows.distinction: Not RenderMarkdown (post-run measured results); a contract or preflight is the credential-gated promise rendered BEFORE any measured number exists.

rows.definition: The renderTUI* family formats one fak console dashboard pane (loops/agent/garden/guard/issues/overview/sessions) from its typed report struct into a fixed-width terminal text view.
rows.distinction: Not the bench RenderMarkdown/contract renderers (which emit Markdown docs); these emit width-bounded terminal panes for the live `fak` console.

rows.definition: nightrun.RenderCapabilities writes the one-line box-capability summary (gpu/weights/datasets/net/creds) that prefixes every nightrun human view; siblings RenderNext and RenderLedger write the focused next-task answer and the durable collection ledger.
rows.distinction: Not the fak-console renderTUI* panes; these are the `fak nightrun` operator text views over the local data-collection queue.

rows.definition: guardrsi.RenderIteration formats one guard-verdict-RSI iteration (rows folded, verdict-quality delta, honesty holes, candidate lever, keep/revert rule) as text; sibling RenderScorecard formats the guard-RSI scorecard payload.
rows.distinction: Not the nightrun or console renderers; these print the guard RSI self-improvement loop's per-iteration and scorecard summaries.

rows.definition: repoguard.renderReason builds the human refusal message when a destructive/write op targets a path outside the repo, listing each violating op->target with its resolved path and the remediation.
rows.distinction: Not gateway.renderVerdict (which serializes a kernel verdict to the wire); renderReason composes the repo-guard's out-of-workspace refusal prose.

rows.definition: render is the repo-wide convention for a pure projection function that turns a typed value into a human/Markdown/wire/terminal text surface (e.g. the bare `func render` in loophealth and dispatchworker), without mutating its input.
rows.distinction: Not materialize (which pages real bytes/spans/tensors into a live store or context); render only formats an already-decided value into text.
